content.body: At the end of https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncblockstart, there's a Note about the possible sources of `_result_`. What I don't understand is, if it's coming from Await, how do you know it's `~unused~`? (Seems more like it would have to be an ES lang value.)
content.formatted_body: At the end of https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-asyncblockstart, there's a Note about the possible sources of _result_. What I don't understand is, if it's coming from Await, how do you know it's ~unused~? (Seems more like it would have to be an ES lang value.)

content.body: it's actually `empty` not `unused` afaict
but by "coming from Await", it means coming from (what is now) RunCallerContext step 4
which Await invokes in its step 8, passing `empty` https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/control-abstraction-objects.html#await
content.formatted_body: which Await invokes in its step 8, passing empty https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/control-abstraction-objects.html#await

content.body: I think Await should probably be passing `unused` there
content.formatted_body: I think Await should probably be passing unused there

content.body: it used to in the 2022 edition, got changed sometime between then and 2023, let me see
AsyncBlockStart used to use `*undefined*`
what I mean is, https://tc39.es/ecma262/2022/multipage/ecmascript-data-types-and-values.html#await step 10 is `unused`
content.formatted_body: what I mean is, https://tc39.es/ecma262/2022/multipage/ecmascript-data-types-and-values.html#await step 10 is unused

content.body: whereas https://tc39.es/ecma262/2023/multipage/control-abstraction-objects.html#await step 10 passes `empty`
changed in https://github.com/tc39/ecma262/pull/2665
Let me see...

When AsyncBlockStart invokes RunSuspendedContext, there's a transfer of control to asyncContext, and this note is saying that the only way control can transfer back is via Await's call to RunCallerContext, or via completion of the closure above (if there's no call to Await).

* When AsyncBlockStart invokes RunSuspendedContext, there's a transfer of control to asyncContext, and this note is saying that the only way control can transfer back is via Await's call to RunCallerContext, or via completion of the closure above (if there's no call to Await)?

m.new_content.body: When AsyncBlockStart invokes RunSuspendedContext, there's a transfer of control to asyncContext, and this note is saying that the only way control can transfer back is via Await's call to RunCallerContext, or via completion of the closure above (if there's no call to Await)?

content.body: correct
and that in both cases, the value which is passed when doing that transfer of control is supposed to be `unused`, except that in Await it's actually `empty` as of the above PR
content.formatted_body: and that in both cases, the value which is passed when doing that transfer of control is supposed to be unused, except that in Await it's actually empty as of the above PR

content.body: What prevents some other call to RunCallerContext?
the only other caller is Yield, I think? and, uh, probably nothing, I suspect that sentence predates async generators
(and so became incorrect when it was added)
oh, no, async generators use AsyncGeneratorStart
so, what prevents it is that the only callers are Await and Yield (+variants), and AsyncFunctionStart is not used to invoke things which can Yield
* so, what prevents it is that the only callers of RunCallerContext are Await and Yield (+variants), and AsyncFunctionStart is not used to invoke things which can Yield

m.new_content.body: so, what prevents it is that the only callers of RunCallerContext are Await and Yield (+variants), and AsyncFunctionStart is not used to invoke things which can Yield

content.body:
RunCallerContext is invoked by GeneratorYield, AsyncGeneratorYield, and Await. So is it true that
- GeneratorStart's closure can only call RCC via GeneratorYield,
- AsyncGeneratorStart's closure can only call RCC via AsyncGeneratorYield, and
- AsyncBlockStart's closure can only call RCC via Await
?
content.formatted_body:
RunCallerContext is invoked by GeneratorYield, AsyncGeneratorYield, and Await. So is it true that

  • GeneratorStart's closure can only call RCC via GeneratorYield,
  • AsyncGeneratorStart's closure can only call RCC via AsyncGeneratorYield, and
  • AsyncBlockStart's closure can only call RCC via Await
    ?

content.body: yes
or, no
AsyncGeneratorStart can also call it via Await
but otherwise yes
ok
AsyncBlockStart's closure can result in more than one call to Await, right?
correct
but only the first one returns to AsyncBlockStart
subsequent ones return control to... whatever transferred control to them in the first place, which is usually the promise machinery I believe
* subsequent ones return control to... whatever transferred control to them in the first place, which is usually (possibly always?) the promise machinery I believe

m.new_content.body: subsequent ones return control to... whatever transferred control to them in the first place, which is usually (possibly always?) the promise machinery I believe

content.body: hm.
And is that machinery prepared to receive `~unused~` from Await's RCC?

content.body: well, it already receives ~unused~ from AsyncBlockStart step 1.i (assuming there was at least one Await), so I would hope so

oh, it's just the Await steps 2.b and 5.b
which ignore it
> b. NOTE: The Completion Record returned by RunSuspendedContext is intentionally ignored.
content.formatted_body: b. NOTE: The Completion Record returned by RunSuspendedContext is intentionally ignored.

content.body: so, yes, they are indeed so prepared
I'm looking at the return types of RunCallerContext and RunSuspendedContext, which is the type that can be "passed back" by each's transfer of control.
Or *should* be that type, but isn't currently.
content.formatted_body: Or should be that type, but isn't currently.

content.body: For RunSuspendedContext, it looks like the proper return type is a throw comp or a normal comp containing ES lang value or ~empty~ or ~unused~.
For RunCallerContext, throw comp or return comp or normal comp containing ES lang value or ~empty~
afk
that sounds right, though presumably Await should be fixed so it uses `unused` instead of `empty`, and then RunSuspendedContext would no longer include Normal Completion containing `empty`, yes?
